heading: Index of legally marketed unapproved new animal drugs for minor species
label: 360ccc–1 Index of legally marketed unapproved new animal drugs for minor species

children.label: A
children.text: new animal drugs intended for use in a minor species for which there is a reasonable certainty that the animal or edible products from the animal will not be consumed by humans or food-producing animals; and

children.label: B
children.text: new animal drugs intended for use only in a hatchery, tank, pond, or other similar contained man-made structure in an early, non-food life stage of a food-producing minor species, where safety for humans is demonstrated in accordance with the standard of (including, for an antimicrobial new animal drug, with respect to antimicrobial resistance).section 360b(d) of this title
The index shall not include a new animal drug that is contained in or a product of a transgenic animal.

children.heading: Conferences
children.label: b Conferences
children.text: Any person intending to file a request under this section shall be entitled to one or more conferences to discuss the requirements for indexing a new animal drug.

children.heading: Request for determination of eligibility for inclusion in index
children.label: c Request for determination of eligibility for inclusion in index
A
children.text: information regarding the need for the new animal drug, the species for which the new animal drug is intended, the proposed intended use and conditions of use, and anticipated annual distribution;

children.label: B
children.text: information to support the conclusion that the proposed use meets the conditions of subparagraph (A) or (B) of subsection (a)(1) of this section;

children.label: C
children.text: information regarding the components and composition of the new animal drug;

children.label: D
children.text: a description of the methods used in, and the facilities and controls used for, the manufacture, processing, and packing of such new animal drug;

children.label: E
children.text: an environmental assessment that meets the requirements of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 [ et seq.], as amended, and as defined in 21 CFR Part 25, as it appears on , and amended thereafter or information to support a categorical exclusion from the requirement to prepare an environmental assessment;42 U.S.C. 4321August 2, 2004

children.label: F
children.text: information sufficient to support the conclusion that the proposed use of the new animal drug is safe under with respect to individuals exposed to the new animal drug through its manufacture or use; andsection 360b(d) of this title

children.label: G
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may deem necessary to make this eligibility determination.

children.label: A
children.text: the same drug in the same dosage form for the same intended use is not approved or conditionally approved;

children.label: B
children.text: the proposed use of the drug meets the conditions of subparagraph (A) or (B) of subsection (a)(1), as appropriate;

children.label: C
children.text: the person requesting the determination has established appropriate specifications for the manufacture and control of the new animal drug and has demonstrated an understanding of the requirements of current good manufacturing practices;

children.label: D
children.text: the new animal drug will not significantly affect the human environment; and

children.label: E
children.text: the new animal drug is safe with respect to individuals exposed to the new animal drug through its manufacture or use.

children.heading: Request for addition to index
children.label: d Request for addition to index
A
children.text: a copy of the Secretary’s determination of eligibility issued under subsection (c);

children.label: B
children.text: a written report that meets the requirements in subsection (d)(2) of this section;

children.label: C
children.text: a proposed index entry;

children.label: D
children.text: facsimile labeling;

children.label: E
children.text: anticipated annual distribution of the new animal drug;

children.label: F
children.text: a written commitment to manufacture the new animal drug and animal feeds bearing or containing such new animal drug according to current good manufacturing practices;

children.label: G
children.text: a written commitment to label, distribute, and promote the new animal drug only in accordance with the index entry;

children.label: H
children.text: upon specific request of the Secretary, information submitted to the expert panel described in paragraph (3); and

children.label: I
children.text: any additional requirements that the Secretary may prescribe by general regulation or specific order.

children.label: A
children.text: be authored by a qualified expert panel;

children.label: B
children.text: include an evaluation of all available target animal safety and effectiveness information, including anecdotal information;

children.label: C
children.text: state the expert panel’s opinion regarding whether the benefits of using the new animal drug for the proposed use in a minor species outweigh its risks to the target animal, taking into account the harm being caused by the absence of an approved or conditionally approved new animal drug for the minor species in question;

children.label: D
children.text: include information from which labeling can be written; and

children.label: E
children.text: include a recommendation regarding whether the new animal drug should be limited to use under the professional supervision of a licensed veterinarian.

children.label: A
children.text: is composed of experts qualified by scientific training and experience to evaluate the target animal safety and effectiveness of the new animal drug under consideration;

children.label: B
children.text: operates external to FDA; and

children.label: C
children.text: is not subject to chapter 10 of title 5.

Within 180 days after the receipt of a request for listing a new animal drug in the index, the Secretary shall grant or deny the request. The Secretary shall grant the request if the request for indexing continues to meet the eligibility criteria in subsection (a) and the Secretary finds, on the basis of the report of the qualified expert panel and other information available to the Secretary, that the benefits of using the new animal drug for the proposed use in a minor species outweigh its risks to the target animal, taking into account the harm caused by the absence of an approved or conditionally-approved new animal drug for the minor species in question. If the Secretary denies the request, the Secretary shall thereafter provide due notice and the opportunity for an informal conference. The decision of the Secretary following an informal conference shall constitute final agency action subject to judicial review.

children.label: A
children.text: the name and address of the person who holds the index listing;

children.label: B
children.text: the name of the drug and the intended use and conditions of use for which it is being indexed;

children.label: C
children.text: product labeling; and

children.label: D
children.text: conditions and any limitations that the Secretary deems necessary regarding use of the drug.
The Secretary shall publish the index, and revise it periodically.
The Secretary may establish by regulation a process for reporting changes in the conditions of manufacturing or labeling of indexed products.

children.heading: Removal from index; suspended listing
children.label: f Removal from index; suspended listing
A
children.text: the expert panel failed to meet the requirements as set forth by the Secretary by regulation;

children.label: B
children.text: on the basis of new information before the Secretary, evaluated together with the evidence available to the Secretary when the new animal drug was listed in the index, the benefits of using the new animal drug for the indexed use do not outweigh its risks to the target animal;

children.label: C
children.text: the conditions of subsection (c)(2) of this section are no longer satisfied;

children.label: D
children.text: the manufacture of the new animal drug is not in accordance with current good manufacturing practices;

children.label: E
children.text: the labeling, distribution, or promotion of the new animal drug is not in accordance with the index entry;

children.label: F
children.text: the conditions and limitations of use associated with the index listing have not been followed; or

children.label: G
children.text: the request for indexing contains any untrue statement of material fact,

children.label: A
children.text: suspend the listing of such drug immediately;

children.label: B
children.text: give the person listed in the index prompt notice of the Secretary’s action; and

children.label: C
children.text: afford that person the opportunity for an informal conference.

children.heading: Regulations concerning exemptions for investigational use
children.label: g Regulations concerning exemptions for investigational use
children.text: For purposes of indexing new animal drugs under this section, to the extent consistent with the public health, the Secretary shall promulgate regulations for exempting from the operation of minor species new animal drugs and animal feeds bearing or containing new animal drugs intended solely for investigational use by experts qualified by scientific training and experience to investigate the safety and effectiveness of minor species animal drugs. Such regulations may, at the discretion of the Secretary, among other conditions relating to the protection of the public health, provide for conditioning such exemption upon the establishment and maintenance of such records, and the making of such reports to the Secretary, by the manufacturer or the sponsor of the investigation of such article, of data (including but not limited to analytical reports by investigators) obtained as a result of such investigational use of such article, as the Secretary finds will enable the Secretary to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of such article in the event of the filing of a request for an index listing pursuant to this section.section 360b of this title

“LEGAL STATUS—In order to be legally marketed, a new animal drug intended for a minor species must be Approved, Conditionally Approved, or Indexed by the Food and Drug Administration. THIS PRODUCT IS INDEXED—MIF #” (followed by the applicable minor species index file number and a period) “Extra-label use is prohibited.”;

except in the case of new animal drugs indexed for use in an early life stage of a food-producing animal, “This product is not to be used in animals intended for use as food for humans or food-producing animals.”; and
such other information as may be prescribed by the Secretary in the index listing.

In the case of any new animal drug for which an index listing pursuant to subsection (a) is in effect, the person who has an index listing shall establish and maintain such records, and make such reports to the Secretary, of data relating to experience, and other data or information, received or otherwise obtained by such person with respect to such drug, or with respect to animal feeds bearing or containing such drug, as the Secretary may by general regulation, or by order with respect to such listing, prescribe on the basis of a finding that such records and reports are necessary in order to enable the Secretary to determine, or facilitate a determination, whether there is or may be ground for invoking subsection (f). Such regulation or order shall provide, where the Secretary deems it to be appropriate, for the examination, upon request, by the persons to whom such regulation or order is applicable, of similar information received or otherwise obtained by the Secretary.

Every person required under this subsection to maintain records, and every person in charge or custody thereof, shall, upon request of an officer or employee designated by the Secretary, permit such officer or employee at all reasonable times to have access to and copy and verify such records.

children.heading: Public disclosure of safety and effectiveness data
children.label: j Public disclosure of safety and effectiveness data
A
children.text: if no work is being or will be undertaken to have the drug indexed in accordance with the request,

children.label: B
children.text: if the Secretary has determined that such drug cannot be indexed and all legal appeals have been exhausted,

children.label: C
children.text: if the indexing of such drug is terminated and all legal appeals have been exhausted, or

children.label: D
children.text: if the Secretary has determined that such drug is not a new animal drug.

children.label: A
children.text: for the purpose of, or as part of a plan, scheme, or device for, obtaining the right to make, use, or market, or making, using, or marketing, outside the United States, the drug identified in the request for indexing; and

children.label: B
children.text: without obtaining from any person to whom the data and information are disclosed an identical verified statement, a copy of which is to be provided by such person to the Secretary, which meets the requirements of this paragraph.

children.heading: Date of determination in the case of recommended controls under the CSA
children.label: k Date of determination in the case of recommended controls under the CSA
children.text: In the case of a request under subsection (d) to add a drug to the index under subsection (a) with respect to a drug for which the Secretary provides notice to the person filing the request that the Secretary intends to issue a scientific and medical evaluation and recommend controls under the Controlled Substances Act [ et seq.], a determination to grant the request to add such drug to the index shall not take effect until the interim final rule controlling the drug is issued in accordance with section 201(j) of the Controlled Substances Act [].21 U.S.C. 80121 U.S.C. 811(j)
